code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Strong dissatisfaction or anger
    - Product is defective, unsafe, completely ineffective, or returned
    - Clear statements such as "terrible," "junk," "don't buy," "returned," "doesn't work"
    - Safety hazards or total failure automatically lean toward 1

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - More negative than positive
    - Significant flaws, poor quality, missing parts, misleading description
    - Functional positives overshadowed by major flaws impacting usability
    - Language such as "cheap," "useless," "not as pictured," "pain to use"

    3 Stars (Neutral / Mixed):
    - Balanced pros and cons
    - "It's okay," "average," "not great but not terrible"
    - Works but with noticeable limitations restricting regular use
    - Conflicted or moderate tone where neither satisfaction nor disappointment dominates

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Mostly positive with minor criticisms
    - Product works well but has clear room for improvement
    - Phrases like "pretty good," "above average," "would recommend with reservations"
    - Minor complaints that don't override core functionality

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - Strong satisfaction and enthusiasm exceeding expectations
    - Clear recommendation to others without reservations
    - Minor drawbacks mentioned but don't diminish transformative benefits
    - Explicit comparisons showing superiority to alternatives
    - Aesthetic and functional praise combined with emotional endorsement

    EDGE CASE HANDLING:
    - Final assessment rule: If review concludes with strong recommendation despite earlier criticisms → 5
    - Multi-aspect reviews: When majority of features excel with one weak area → 5
    - Star deduction mentions: "Deducting one star for X but..." → Analyze remaining sentiment strength
    - Conditional praise: "Great if..." → 3 unless specific to niche use case
    - Comparative value: "Best I've used" → 5 even with minor flaws
    - Safety issues override all positives → 1
    - Sarcasm detection: Look for contradiction between tone and content
</DynamicRules>

</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: It's not a good vacuum. It's hard to pick up dirt and then it will spit it out so you have to go over and over the same area. I wouldn't buy it again.
<Reasoning>Core functionality failure with explicit rejection. Clear 1-star sentiment.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: The blender works okay, but it's much louder than expected and overheats after a few minutes. It does make smooth smoothies, but the noise and overheating make it frustrating to use.
<Reasoning>Functional positives overshadowed by major usability flaws. Overall negative dominates.</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: These are really nice IF you only use them for bigger items. They are too thick and bulky for smaller items.
<Reasoning>Conditional praise with significant limitations. Restricted utility = neutral.</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: I always hate not being able to get the last remains out of a jar. This solves that problem perfectly! Deducting one star because the handle could be more comfortable.
<Reasoning>Minor ergonomic issue doesn't override exceptional problem-solving capability.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: This mattress is amazing! It's comfortable and supportive, though a bit heavy to move. The quality is top-notch, and I sleep much better now.
<Reasoning>Transformative benefits outweigh minor inconvenience. Clear 5-star despite drawback.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: The towels are cute and well-made but barely absorbent. Had to wash twice with fabric softener to get decent performance.
<Reasoning>Aesthetic praise countered by functional deficiency. Mixed with improvement effort.</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: This mattress gets 4 stars for comfort, but there's so many other nice features that it gets 5 stars overall. Perfect for guest rooms!
<Reasoning>Explicit multi-aspect evaluation with positive conclusion. Features outweigh single rating.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: Arrived with scratches and dents. Customer service refused replacement. Complete waste of money.
<Reasoning>Defective product + poor service. Strong negative experience.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (30 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 13
  gold 5 -> 4: 8
  gold 4 -> 3: 6
  gold 1 -> 4: 1
  gold 5 -> 3: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "We are both tough on dish towels and use them constantly. Not only do we cook 90% of our meals at home but we also use fresh fruits and veggies with pretty much every meal and that means a lot of washing, drying and carrying things around...why carry..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  2. Review: "I would consider this 6 piece set to be hand towels. They are 16 inches by 30 inches. They are not the thinnest towels I have ever seen, and they are far from the plushest. I checked the description again and they have bath towels and wash cloths. Th..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 3, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "I like this towel. It is 100% Turkish cotton and measures 27 inches by 55 inches. The dobby weave is somewhat intricate so you are going to get some good absorbency. This towel also comes in a bath sheet, which is competively priced with the local st..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "So far this blender is working well for me. I've made a few strawberry banana smoothies for my kids and it's worked fine each time. One thing that wasn't skimped on was the looks. The build design of these blenders is very sharp looking, having a bri..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "EDIT: A year of use later it's still humming along and doing its job just fine. Sometimes it seems like it doesn't pick up quickly that it should be upping its speed on its own in the auto setting, but maybe it's just taking longer for the air to rea..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "I'm partial to the Presto Pop Lite hot air popper, but it looks cheap and plasticy, so I tried this.

All in all this works like the Presto or any other hot air popper, but it has a couple nuances (good and bad):

This unit is more compact and attrac..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [2, 3, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "This is a coffee pot for coffee geeks.  Most discerning coffee aficionados likely wouldn't notice much if any difference from a a great less complicated coffee make, such as a Bonavita.  To cut to the point, this probably isn't for you, but who knows..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [3, 5, 5] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "Over the past two months or so I've gotten numerous sets of kitchen towels and the process was always the same. Try them. See the weren't absorbent. Wash them numerous times with fabric softener to make them somewhat absorbent and keep at least four ..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "This is a good, and rather useful bin.  It's well made, has sturdy handles, and gets lots of use in our house, which says a lot.  When not in use, it collapses, though again we use it a lot.  Sometimes we put more weight in here that perhaps we shoul..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [4, 5, 5] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 30 errors, 18 borderline out of 80 examples."}
